etymology.description: semantic 亻(人) (rén, "person") + phonetic 弋(弋) (yì) -> representing substitution or replacement among people
etymology.interpretation: The character combines the element 亻(人) (rén, "person") indicating a person or people, with 弋(弋) (yì), which provides the sound. The concept of replacing or succeeding a person is central to the idea of 'generation', as one generation replaces another.

examples.chinese: 这一代的年轻人(這一代的年輕人)喜欢使用科技产品(喜歡使用科技產品)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè yī dài de niánqīng rén xǐhuān shǐyòng kējì chǎnpǐn.
examples.english: This generation of young people likes to use technology products.

examples.chinese: 我们家已经传了好几代(我們家已經傳了好幾代)，每一代都有自己的故事(每一代都有自己的故事)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen jiā yǐjīng chuánle hǎo jǐ dài, měi yī dài dōu yǒu zìjǐ de gùshì.
examples.english: Our family has been passed down for several generations, and each generation has its own story.

examples.chinese: 他将未来的希望寄托在下一代(他將未來的希望寄托在下一代)。
examples.pinyin: Tā jiāng wèilái de xīwàng jìtuō zài xià yī dài.
examples.english: He places his hopes for the future on the next generation.
